commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a signed Ed25519 envelope verifier for hosted official external plugin catalog feeds and unit coverage for accepted and rejected envelopes.

PR surface: Source +209, Tests +206. Total +415 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature-foundation PR, not a reported bug. Source inspection shows current main lacks the envelope verifier and the PR adds isolated unit-covered primitives without runtime wiring.

target.title: feat: add hosted feed envelope verifier
target.body:
Related: https://github.com/openclaw/rfcs/pull/25

## What Problem This Solves

Starts the hosted feed trust-verification implementation series by adding the signed envelope verifier primitives that later feed refresh and source-profile PRs can consume.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds a small, isolated verifier for OpenClaw-hosted external plugin catalog envelopes. It verifies Ed25519 signatures over stable base64url payload bytes, accepts PEM or raw base64url public keys, validates the payload type, and only parses the feed after a trusted signature verifies.

This PR intentionally does not wire signed envelopes into marketplace refresh, config, snapshot persistence, CLI output, or install eligibility. Those stay in follow-up PRs from the RFC trust series.

## User Impact

No user-visible behavior changes yet. This is a draft implementation foundation for future signed hosted feed verification.

## Evidence

```text
> node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  7 passed (7)

> pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

> pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.test.ts
PASS

> git diff --cached --check
PASS
```
